data.narrative:
OroraTech is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised €38M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M). Five public Space & Aerospace comparables were identified (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) but cannot inform valuation without OroraTech's revenue.

The peer-median method yields a $1.3B implied valuation, centered within the observed range of $400M to $2.0B across the three private comparables. This single applicable triangulation method provides the foundation for the analysis. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not feasible; while the Space & Aerospace sector trades at a median of 42.9x revenue across the six public names in scope, OroraTech's revenue is not disclosed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.5B, with a central estimate of $1.3B. The median peer valuation is the primary driver; the range reflects uncertainty inherent in a single-method approach and the wide dispersion ($1.6B gap) among the three peer comparables. Key limitations include the small peer sample size and absence of revenue or operational metrics for OroraTech, which would otherwise enable multiple triangulation methods and tighter confidence bands.
